title: Aureline mutation-journal entry
description: Seed schema for mutation-journal entries. Every mutating command (editor edit, multi-cursor keystroke, structural edit, refactor, AI apply, save-participant group, settings / migration import, external reload, decode recovery, filesystem rename, build or codegen run, preview regeneration, audit-only external-effect) records one entry in this shape so local-history, AI evidence packets, review packs, recovery exports, support bundles, and the eventual replay lane read one lineage vocabulary. The schema reuses ADR-0003 undo classes (docs/adr/0003-buffer-undo-large-file.md) and ADR-0006 filesystem-identity records (docs/adr/0006-vfs-save-cache-identity.md); it does not redefine them. Rust types in the eventual mutation-journal crate are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool export that external readers (support-bundle exporter, replay capture, CLI doctor, companion surfaces) rely on. The reversal-class axis (exact_undo, compensating_undo, regenerate_or_recompute, restore_from_checkpoint, audit_only) is orthogonal to the ADR-0003 undo-class axis and is carried on every entry so consumers can distinguish 'what the editor called it' from 'what a user can actually get back'.

mutation_journal_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the mutation-journal payload shape. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape.

mutation_id.description: Stable per-mutation identifier allocated before commit. Never reused. Support bundles, review packs, AI evidence packets, and local-history entries reference this value rather than duplicating the entry body.

group_id.description: Stable group identifier shared across a named operation (refactor apply, AI patch, multi-file rename, format-on-save plus participants, scaffolding run, migration import, bulk replace, generated-artifact refresh). One operation, one group_id, many mutation_ids.

command_id.description: Canonical command identifier that originated the mutation. Resolves through the command plane; supports cross-surface attribution and replay.

actor_class.description: Who or what produced the mutation. Determines which actor_ref fields are required and how the entry renders in attribution surfaces.

source_class.description: Provenance class of the mutation source. The support bundle and the AI-evidence lane quote this verbatim; it is never inferred from actor_class alone.

display_name.description: Human-readable attribution label (user handle, agent name, generator identity, formatter identity, model identity). Redaction policy may replace this with an opaque label in exported bundles.

stable_id.description: Optional stable identifier (session id, agent id, provider:model id, generator id). Opaque to this schema.

role.description: Optional role tag (author, reviewer, operator, automation) for audit surfaces.

actor_ref.description: Actor attribution. Redaction-aware; the exporter is responsible for applying the redaction policy.

scope_ref.description: Typed scope within which the mutation applied. Aligns with the ADR-0005 subscription scope vocabulary plus the ADR-0003 / ADR-0006 buffer-and-file scopes.

filesystem_identity.description: Full filesystem-identity record (ADR 0006 layers 1-4) for filesystem / buffer / generated-artifact targets. Required when target_kind is filesystem_object, buffer, or generated_artifact.

logical_ref.description: Opaque logical reference for non-filesystem targets (setting key, task id, service endpoint). The mutation-journal consumer interprets this in context.

affected_range.description: Optional affected range inside the target for fine-grained entries. Line / byte coordinates follow the ADR-0003 buffer coordinate system.

target_ref.description: One target of the mutation. A single entry may carry many targets when the mutation spans them.

undo_class.description: ADR-0003 undo-class id. Rides unchanged into the mutation journal; the compensation posture of the class (compensatable vs only_revertible) is not restated here because it is invariant and lives in artifacts/architecture/undo_class_rows.yaml.

reversal_class.description: What a user can actually get back. Orthogonal to undo_class. The UI MUST surface this value verbatim; 'Undo' with no class context is forbidden on compensating / regenerate / audit-only mutations. TAD Appendix DD §DD.2.

reversible.description: Convenience flag: true when reversal_class is anything other than audit_only and no blocking drift is declared. Consumers that render a simple badge read this; consumers that render the action read reversal_class.

declared_at_commit.description: Whether the reversal class shown here was declared at commit time. If authority state has since drifted and the original exact_undo is now compensating, an amendment record flips this to false and cites the drift via downgrade_reason.

downgrade_reason.description: Optional reason explaining why a mutation's reversal class was downgraded after commit (external_change_detected, generator_changed, policy_epoch_rolled, ...). Quoted verbatim by support bundles.

reversibility.description: Reversible / irreversible flag set. The ADR-0003 rule 'if an operation was exact at execution time but authority drifted, downgrade the advertised reversal path visibly' is encoded here.

durability_class.description: Mirrors ADR-0006 cache-identity durability_class. Durable checkpoints survive process restart and may be cited by restore_from_checkpoint reversal.

checkpoint_ref.description: Link to a checkpoint the reversal or audit path may use. Mutations MAY reference many checkpoints (one save manifest, one recovery-journal checkpoint, one preview snapshot).

preview_ref.description: Link to the preview record that produced the mutation. Required for any grouped apply path that is required by policy to be previewable.

approval_policy.description: Policy id that required the approval (review-required-before-save, admin-approval, AI-evidence-gate). Opaque to this schema.

save_manifest_ref.description: Opaque reference to an ADR-0006 save_manifest record. Set on mutations that reached the save pipeline.

generated_artifact_lineage_ref.description: Opaque reference to a generated-artifact lineage record (see generated_artifact_lineage.schema.json). Set on mutations that mutated a generated artifact (build output, codegen output, lockfile, notebook output, preview snapshot, mirrored pack).

policy_epoch.description: Policy-epoch identifier in effect at commit. Replay consumers compare this against the current epoch before re-running any compensating command.

redaction_class.description: Redaction class for the journal body when exported to a support bundle, AI evidence packet, or replay capture. Aligned with the TAD privacy-review workflow.

summary.description: Short redaction-aware human-readable summary (≤ 1024 graphemes). Product, CLI, and support surfaces render the same text.

external_targets.description: Opaque list of external targets the mutation touched (git remote, registry, API endpoint). Used by audit_only reversal-class entries.

semantic_readiness_ref.description: Optional semantic-readiness frame for the producer (AI, generator, refactor engine) that emitted the mutation. Carries the frozen readiness state, not-ready reason, and safe-next-action when the producer was less than exact at commit time.

durable_vs_disposable.description: State-class of the authority the mutation wrote into. 'durable_user_authored' covers buffers, settings, profiles, snippets; 'durable_workspace_authored' covers workspace manifests, task configs, launch configs; 'disposable_derived' covers derived indexes, caches, preview bundles. The mutation journal MUST classify so clear-caches flows never blow away a durable_user_authored entry.

started_at.description: Producer-local monotonic timestamp at which the mutation began. Opaque to this schema.

committed_at.description: Producer-local monotonic timestamp at which the commit point was reached. Equals started_at for single-step mutations.

mutation_journal_entry.description: One mutation-journal entry. Every mutating command writes exactly one entry to the journal; grouped operations also write a mutation_group_record.

group_kind.description: Named group class. Consumers render the group-class label directly; they never derive it from member classes.

member_mutation_ids.description: IDs of every mutation_journal_entry in this group. Support bundles cite the group record and resolve members by id.

mutation_group_record.description: One mutation-group record. A named operation opens one group, emits member mutation_journal_entry records, and closes with a resolution. The group's reversal_class is the most restrictive of its members (any audit_only member forces the group to audit_only).
